data.title: Is Netflix Stock (NFLX) a Buy Ahead of Q4 Earnings?
data.description:
Streaming giant Netflix (NFLX) is set to report its fourth quarter fiscal 2025 results after market close on Tuesday, January 20. Analysts expect a modest rise in earnings and sales, driven by steady subscriber growth in international markets. Netflix could also announce price hikes for its subscription plans to boost revenues amid U.S. subscriber weakness.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Moreover, investors will focus on management's insights on user engagement, advertising revenue, profitability margins, upcoming content slate, and fiscal 2026 guidance. NFLX stock has gained just 4.5% over the past year, lagging the broader market. It shed most gains after missing Q3 FY25 earnings and sales estimates due to a one-time $619 million charge from a Brazilian tax settlement.

Expectations from Netflix

Analysts and investors will watch for updates on the pending takeover of Warner Bros. Discovery's (WBD) studios and streaming assets. Rival Paramount Skydance's (PSKY) hostile bids for all of WBD, plus ongoing legal battles, have delayed the deal.

The Street expects Netflix to report a nearly 28% year-over-year jump in earnings per share (EPS) to $0.55. Also, sales are projected to rise 16.7% to $11.97 billion. Importantly, Netflix has exceeded Wall Street's expectations in six of its last eight quarters, reflecting continued momentum.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: 'Green Light Ahead!' Top Analyst Upgrades NIO Stock with a 31% Upside Target
data.description:
Top Macquarie analyst Eugene Hsiao has turned bullish on Chinese EV maker Nio Inc. (NIO), upgrading the stock rating to Buy from Hold. Also, the analyst lifted the price target to $6.10 (31% upside potential), up from $5.30, citing stronger-than-expected vehicle demand and a more supportive outlook for volumes.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

It is worth noting that Hsiao ranks 992 out of more than 12,000 analysts tracked by TipRanks. He has a success rate of 71%, with an average return per rating of 28.3% over a one-year timeframe.

Why This Analyst Sees More Upside for Nio

To begin with, Hsiao highlighted that NIO ended the fourth quarter at the top end of its delivery guidance, reaching around 125,000 vehicles. Deliveries jumped 44% quarter over quarter, driven mainly by solid demand for the ES8 model and the Firefly lineup.

The analyst sees this rebound as an early sign that demand is stabilizing after a tough period for China's EV market.

Another key positive is the growing use of NIO's Battery-as-a-Service (BaaS) option. More than 80% of buyers are now choosing this model, according to the analyst. Hsiao believes this helps shield NIO from rising battery costs, since the batteries remain off the company's balance sheet. This could ease margin pressure at a time when the broader EV sector is facing cost uncertainty.

Based on stronger ES8 and Firefly demand, Macquarie raised its FY26 vehicle volume forecast by 7% to 451,000 units. At the same time, the firm acknowledged that challenges remain. It still expects margin pressure and a larger net loss in FY26.

However, Hsiao believes NIO can still deliver close to 40% volume growth, which would allow the company to gain market share even in a weak China EV market.

Is Nio a Buy, Sell, or Hold Now? 

Overall, Wall Street has a Hold consensus rating on NIO stock based on three Buys, three Holds, and one Sell assigned in the last three months. The average NIO stock price target of $6.03 implies 29.96% upside potential from current levels.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: BBC Set to Team Up with YouTube in New Content Deal
data.description:
The BBC is reportedly teaming up with Google (GOOGL)-owned YouTube in a landmark content deal that will bring the broadcaster's programs to the platform for the first time. According to the Financial Times, the BBC will create original shows specifically for YouTube, which will later also appear on the broadcaster's iPlayer and Sounds platforms. Sources say the deal could be officially announced as soon as next week.

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The partnership is a big move for the BBC to reach audiences around the world online and adjust to changing viewing habits, as U.S. tech platforms compete for viewers.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Netflix, Sony Expand Partnership with a Major Exclusive Global Pay-1 Deal
data.description:
Netflix (NFLX) and Sony Pictures Entertainment, a subsidiary of the Japanese conglomerate Sony Group (SONY), are expanding their partnership with a new Pay‑1 licensing deal that will bring Sony's theatrical films to Netflix worldwide after they leave theaters. With this deal, Netflix becomes the exclusive Pay-1 streaming home of Sony Pictures' feature films.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

It is worth noting that the Pay‑1 window refers to the first 18‑month period after a film leaves theaters and digital rental platforms.

As per the agreed terms, Netflix will also license select titles from Sony's extensive film and television library, adding even more depth to the platform's catalog.

The rollout will begin later in 2026, as regional rights expire, with full worldwide availability expected by early 2029. Importantly, the deal extends through 2032.

This new agreement builds on the companies' existing U.S. and regional Pay‑1 agreements, and for the first time, sends all of Sony's theatrical films to Netflix worldwide.

Here's Why This Deal Matters 

For Netflix, the deal gives a steady pipeline of big theatrical films at a time when competition for top content is heating up. Sony, which does not operate its own streaming service, gains a strong global distribution partner and a profitable way to release its movies without building a platform itself.

Netflix executives said the deal brings "incredible value" to subscribers, who regularly push Sony movies into the platform's global Top 10 lists.

Sony added that the expanded partnership points to the lasting strength of its theatrical films and the benefits of staying independent instead of running its own streaming service.

Who Is Better, NFLX or SONY?

data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: One Buy! Cathie Wood Wraps Up Thursday with a Single Biotech Purchase
data.description:
Cathie Wood's ARK Invest ETFs (exchange-traded funds) made just one trade on Thursday, January 15, according to its daily fund disclosures. The firm added to its position in gene-editing company Intellia Therapeutics (NTLA), with no other buys or sells reported for the day.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

On January 15, ARK purchased a total of 56,769 shares of Intellia across its two ETFs. The ARK Innovation ETF (ARKK) added 48,178 shares, while the ARK Genomic Revolution ETF (ARKG) bought 8,591 shares. The combined value of the trades came to about $682,000. 

Is Intellia a Good Stock to Buy?

Intellia stock has gained about 26% over the past year, helped by steady progress in its gene-editing trials and growing investor interest. The company continues to advance its lead CRISPR-based programs through clinical trials, which analysts say is an important step toward future sales.

The latest purchase follows steady accumulation earlier in the week, signaling continued confidence in Intellia's long-term outlook. Earlier this week, ARK added 99,292 shares of Intellia on Monday, followed by another 40,067 shares on Tuesday.

According to TipRanks, NTLA stock has received a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on nine Buys, 12 Holds, and one Sell assigned in the past three months. At $16.41, the average NTLA stock price target implies a 38.25% upside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Michael Burry Sounds the Alarm on Payments Sector – XYZ, PYPL, FOUR
data.description:
The Big Short investor Michael Burry published an 8,000-word analysis on the payments sector, with a focus on Block (XYZ), PayPal (PYPL), and Shift4 (FOUR). He argued that the sector exhibits 'minimal organic growth' and relies on acquisition-driven expansion, in his Substack post.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Burry contends that several firms in the space use "accounting tricks and misrepresentations" to inflate earnings, making much of the sector a high-risk, "too hard" universe for long-term value investors.

These Are Burry's Key Findings

  • Minimal organic growth – Burry noted that growth within payments companies largely stems from acquisitions rather than internal expansion. This pattern signals structural barriers to sustained revenue and margin growth.
  • Acquisition-driven growth and market skepticism – Burry believes that the stock market disfavors growth stories built on frequent acquisitions. They are viewed as signs of slow organic progress and potential overpayment for targets.
  • Accounting and transparency concerns – Burry alleges that certain firms use "M&A accounting tricks and misrepresentations" to bolster earnings and mask weak profitability and cash flow.

Burry's Take on Payments Companies

PayPal – Burry expresses skepticism regarding PayPal's pricing power and corporate culture, suggesting that reported earnings exceed sustainable cash generation. He stated that PYPL is incompatible with his value-investing framework and heightens risks to long-term shareholder value.

Shift4 – Burry stated that he would consider the stock at $30 per share, which implies a heavy discount from the current trading level of $65.91 per share. He cited governance concerns but also acknowledged that Shift4's internal R&D efforts are not without merit, though they may not offset broader structural risk.

Block – Burry acknowledged that Block has certain favorable aspects and credible management. He warns that the remaining uncertainties and details do not present a compelling value proposition for him at current levels. For context, XYZ stock is trading close to $65 per share, at a major discount to its all-time high of $94.25.

Ending Thoughts

To conclude, Burry advises investors to label the payments sector in the "too hard" pile and pivot elsewhere, given the sector's heavy reliance on acquisition-fueled growth strategy that undermines sustainable value creation.

We used the TipRanks Stock Comparison Tool to see how these companies perform on different parameters. Currently, Wall Street has assigned a "Strong Buy" consensus rating to Block stock, while they remain cautiously optimistic about Shift4 and stay sidelined on PayPal.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Stock Market Today: SPY, QQQ Climb on U.S.-Taiwan Trade Deal; Easing Jobless Claims
data.description:
The S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) finished Thursday in positive territory, although both ETFs fell from their intraday highs during the last two hours of the trading session.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In a sign of a resilient labor market, initial jobless claims for the week ended January 9 decreased by 9,000 to 198,000, below the consensus estimate of 215,000. Continuing jobless claims, which trail initial claims by a week, fell by 19,000 to 1.884 million and were also below the expectation of 1.897 million. Initial claims serve as a gauge of layoffs, while continuing claims track the number of people receiving unemployment benefits.

Meanwhile, the U.S. and Taiwan have agreed to a trade deal that will see the self-governing island's tech firms commit to investing at least $250 billion into semiconductor, AI, and energy operations in the U.S. In addition, the Taiwanese government will provide a minimum of $250 billion in credit guarantees to Taiwanese companies for investing in the U.S. semiconductor ecosystem. In return, the U.S. will drop Taiwan's tariff rate to 15% from 20% and exempt generic pharmaceuticals, the ingredients used in those drugs, aircraft components, and some natural resources from duties.

Elsewhere, two Fed officials warned of steep consequences if the Fed loses its independence amid a Justice Department probe into Fed Chair Jerome Powell over the central bank's $2.5 billion renovation of its Washington, D.C., headquarters. "Anything that's infringing or attacking the independence of the central bank is a mess," Chicago Federal Reserve President Austan Goolsbee told CNBC's "Squawk Box." "You're going to get inflation come roaring back if you try to take away the independence of the central bank."

Furthermore, Federal Reserve Governor Michael Barr framed the investigation as an "assault on the independence of the Fed." Barr stressed that the Fed's monetary policy decisions take into account inflation and the labor market while casting aside political biases.

Finally, President Trump is considering invoking the Insurrection Act to address mass protests against Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) agents in Minnesota. The federal law allows the president to deploy the U.S. military domestically to help suppress unrest, insurrection, or rebellion. Earlier this week, Minnesota Governor Tim Walz accused the Trump administration of "organized brutality" after an ICE agent fatally shot 37-year-old Minneapolis resident Renee Good.

The S&P 500 (SPX) closed with a 0.26% gain, while the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) returned 0.32%.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Verizon's $9.6B Frontier Deal Gets Final California Green Light
data.description:
Verizon Communications (VZ) has cleared the final regulatory hurdle for its $9.6 billion acquisition of Frontier (FYBR), securing approval from California regulators after agreeing to a set of concessions. The acquisition is now expected to close on January 20, paving the way for Verizon to expand its fiber footprint in key U.S. markets.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Approval of the Frontier deal could help ease some of the pressure on VZ stock. Verizon shares recently came under pressure after a major outage affected about 180,000 customers. The company is offering $20 credits to impacted users as compensation.

More Details About the Deal

On Thursday, the California Public Utilities Commission unanimously approved the deal. As part of the agreement, Verizon pledged to invest $500 million over the next five years with small businesses in California, expand its network across the state, and provide discounted phone and internet plans to eligible residents.

Verizon agreed to buy Frontier for $9.6 billion in September 2024, but the deal faced regulatory hurdles. It won FCC approval last year after rolling back some diversity policies, while California raised concerns over compliance with its own supplier diversity rules.

Following the completion of the deal, Frontier's common stock will be delisted from Nasdaq and deregistered. Trading in Frontier shares is expected to end on January 16.

What the Frontier Deal Means for Verizon

The Frontier acquisition will combine Frontier's fiber-optic internet network with Verizon's nationwide 5G mobile service, creating a stronger, more integrated connectivity platform.

Once the deal closes, Verizon's fiber network will reach nearly 30 million locations across 31 states and Washington, D.C. This expanded footprint is expected to speed up Verizon's national fiber rollout, strengthen its broadband business, and improve its ability to compete at scale across the U.S.

Verizon CEO Dan Schulman said closing the Frontier deal is a major milestone for the company to regain market leadership.

Is Verizon Stock a Good Buy?

On TipRanks, VZ stock has a consensus Moderate Buy rating based on four Buys and 10 Holds assigned in the last three months. Meanwhile, Verizon's average price target of $46.25 implies a 17.5% upside from current levels.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Why Is Penny Stock Beyond Meat (BYND) Up Over 8% Today?
data.description:
Beyond Meat (BYND), a penny stock, moved over 8% in Thursday's trading after the plant-based food maker announced plans to enter the beverage space with a new protein-infused sparkling drink. The price hike reflects that investors appear encouraged by Beyond Meat's willingness to explore new categories as its core business remains under pressure.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In its most recent quarterly results, Beyond Meat reported a sharp drop in revenue, underlining the need for new growth drivers. Sales fell 13.3% year over year to $70.22 million, while earnings per share came in at -$0.47, missing analysts' consensus estimate of -$0.40.

Beyond Meat Tests a New Growth Idea

Beyond Meat is launching Beyond Immerse, a flavored sparkling beverage made with plant-based protein. The drinks offer either 10 grams or 20 grams of protein, along with fiber, antioxidants, and electrolytes. The company is pitching the product as a lighter and easier option than heavy protein shakes, aimed at consumers who want protein without a thick texture or strong taste.

The drinks will be sold for a limited time starting January 15 through Beyond Meat's Beyond Test Kitchen platform. The lineup includes three flavors: peach mango, lemon lime, and orange tangerine. A limited release allows the company to test demand before committing to a wider launch.

Is BYND Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Sell consensus rating on BYND stock based on one Hold and three Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average BYND price target of $0.93 per share implies 10.26% downside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: U.S. Futures Rise as TSMC, NVDA, and Bank Stocks Drive Gains
data.description:
U.S. stock futures moved higher late Thursday as gains in banks and chip stocks lifted market mood. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 (SPX) Index were up 0.25%, 0.06%, and 0.15%, respectively, at 9:12 p.m. EDT on January 15.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In yesterday's trading session, U.S. stocks finished higher across the board. The S&P 500 and the Nasdaq Composite both rose close to 0.3%, while the Dow gained a stronger 0.6%. Small-cap stocks led the move, with the Russell 2000 climbing nearly 0.9%.

Chip stocks stood out after Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) posted strong fourth-quarter results. The upbeat report revived hopes around the AI trade. Taiwan Semi shares jumped more than 4%, while Nvidia (NVDA) and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) each gained around 2%.

Banks also helped push the market higher. Goldman Sachs (GS) and Morgan Stanley (MS) both reported solid quarterly results. Goldman shares rose more than 4%, while Morgan Stanley climbed nearly 6%.

Even with Thursday's gains, investors are heading into the end of a busy week on a cautious note. The major indexes are still on track for weekly losses, with the S&P 500 down about 0.3%, the Nasdaq off 0.6%, and the Dow lower by roughly 0.1% so far this week.
